input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-06T00:51:58.885Z</current_datetime>

We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user migrated from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform and is getting exit code 8 when test assemblies produce zero tests. This is a known behavioral difference - Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns a non-zero exit code (8) when no tests are discovered, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their test project to understand the setup and provide the fix.

toolResult.sessionLog: Fixing zero-test exit code

result.detailedContent: Fixing zero-test exit code

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

result.detailedContent:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
 <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
   <PropertyGroup>
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
Exit code 8 means "no tests discovered." Microsoft.Testing.Platform treats zero tests as an error by default, unlike VSTest.

**Fix:** Add the `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` property to your `.csproj`:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation excludes all tests) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation excludes all tests) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args:
{"path": "/tmp/sv-c95cf4e1d6124dc6943205a0e66def19/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation excludes all tests) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation excludes all tests) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Unknown option '--nologo'
Usage TestProject [option providers] [extension option providers]

Execute a .NET Test Application.

Options:
    --config-file
        Specifies a testconfig.json file.

    --diagnostic
        Enable the diagnostic logging. The default log level is 'Trace'.
        The file will be written in the output directory with the name log_[yyMMddHHmmssfff].diag

    --diagnostic-filelogger-synchronouswrite
        Force the built-in file logger to write the log synchronously.
        Useful for scenario where you don't want to lose any log (i.e. in case of crash).
        Note that this is slowing down the test execution.

    --diagnostic-output-directory
        Output directory of the diagnostic logging.
        If not specified the file will be generated inside the default 'TestResults' directory.

    --diagnostic-output-fileprefix
        Prefix for the log file name that will replace '[log]_.'

    --diagnostic-verbosity
        Define the level of the verbosity for the --diagnostic.
        The available values are 'Trace', 'Debug', 'Information', 'Warning', 'Error', and 'Critical'.

    --exit-on-process-exit
        Exit the test process if dependent process exits. PID must be provided.

    --help
        Show the command line help.

    --ignore-exit-code
        Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes
        (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)

    --info
        Display .NET test application information.

    --list-tests
        List available tests.

    --minimum-expected-tests
        Specifies the minimum number of tests that are expected to run.

    --results-directory
        The directory where the test results are going to be placed.
        If the specified directory doesn't exist, it's created.
        The default is TestResults in the directory that contains the test application.

    --timeout
        A global test execution timeout.
        Takes one argument as string in the format <value>[h|m|s] where 'value' is float.


Extension options:
    --coverage
        Collect the code coverage using dotnet-coverage tool

    --coverage-output
        Output file

    --coverage-output-format
        Output file format. Supported values: 'coverage', 'xml' and 'cobertura'

    --coverage-settings
        XML code coverage settings

    --filter
        Filters tests using the given expression. For more information, see the Filter option details section. For more information and examples on how to use selective unit test filtering, see https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/selective-unit-tests.

    --maximum-failed-tests
        Specifies a maximum number of test failures that, when exceeded, will abort the test run.

    --no-ansi
        Disable outputting ANSI escape characters to screen.

    --no-progress
        Disable reporting progress to screen.

    --output
        Output verbosity when reporting tests.
        Valid values are 'Normal', 'Detailed'. Default is 'Normal'.

    --report-trx
        Enable generating TRX report

    --report-trx-filename
        The name of the generated TRX report

    --settings
        The path, relative or absolute, to the .runsettings file. For more information and examples on how to configure test run, see https://learn.microsoft.com/visualstudio/test/configure-unit-tests-by-using-a-dot-runsettings-file#the-runsettings-file

    --test-parameter
        Specify or override a key-value pair parameter. For more information and examples, see https://learn.microsoft.com/visualstudio/test/configure-unit-tests-by-using-a-dot-runsettings-file#testrunparameters



Exit code: 5
<exited with exit code 0>

data.reasoningText:
The `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` MSBuild property didn't seem to work. Let me check the log file to see what exit code the runner actually returned, and also check if this is the right property name. 

Looking at the help output, I notice: `--ignore-exit-code` option: "Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes (e.g. '--ignore-exit-code 8;9' ignore exit code 8 and 9 and will return 0 in these case)"

So one approach is using `--ignore-exit-code 8` on the command line.

But the MSBuild property approach should also work. 

I need to verify the exact property name for the Microsoft.Testing.Platform, since it might be named differently than what I'm using. Let me check the actual test logs and the documentation to confirm what the correct MSBuild property should be. Looking at the runner's help output, I see:
- `--minimum-expected-tests`: "Specifies the minimum number of tests that are expected to run."
- `--ignore-exit-code`: "Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes"

The MSBuild property to pass `--ignore-exit-code 8` would be different. Let me check if `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` is the right approach, or if I should use a different property.

Actually, looking at Microsoft documentation more carefully, I believe the correct approach for newer versions is to use the `--minimum-expected-tests 0` command line option or set `<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--minimum-expected-tests 0</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>` as an MSBuild property.

For more recent versions of Microsoft.Testing.Platform, exit code 8 indicates no tests were found, and I can suppress it either via the `--ignore-exit-code 8` command line flag or through an MSBuild property. The property might be `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` set to `0`, though this could be version-dependent. Looking at the test output showing "Failed: 0, Passed: 0, Skipped: 0, Total: 0", it seems no tests were discovered since the slow tests flag isn't enabled. I'm realizing `dotnet test` might be handling the exit code separately from the testing platform runner itself. Let me try passing `--ignore-exit-code 8` directly through the command line with `dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8`, or find the right MSBuild property to set this behavior in the project file.

data.reasoningText:
The MSBuild property `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` doesn't exist in the targets. The only way to suppress exit code 8 is through the `--ignore-exit-code 8` command-line argument. 

For `dotnet test`, you can pass this via `-- --ignore-exit-code 8`. But to make it permanent in the project, we should use a `testingplatformconfig.json` or similar configuration file, or we can use the `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` property.

Let me check if there's a way to configure this via a config file or MSBuild property that passes command-line arguments.

<!-- Return exit code 0 when no tests are discovered (e.g., conditional compilation excludes all tests) -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Treat 'no tests discovered' (exit code 8) as success for conditional-compilation scenarios -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content: Both scenarios work. Here's the final project file:

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
5.     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
6.     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
7.     <!-- Treat 'no tests discovered' (exit code 8) as success for conditional-compilation scenarios -->
8.     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
9.   </PropertyGroup>
10.   <ItemGroup>
11.     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
12.   </ItemGroup>
13. </Project>
14.

data.content:
**The fix:** Add this property to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>--ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
```

**What's happening:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns exit code **8** when zero tests are discovered (unlike VSTest which returned 0). The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag tells the runner to treat that as success. Actual test failures (exit code 2) will still fail your CI correctly.
